transcript:
Watch the red circles at the right edge of each line. They stay hollow rings while a sentence is actively typing — then, only after the cursor has already jumped down to the next line, the ring slowly fills in with red. That's not a bug in the timing, it's the whole piece.

In the code, that fill is controlled by a variable called "witnessed by no one," and it only increments for lines where the index is less than "where generation happens" — meaning the sentence being completed is never the one currently being written. The comment right above it says the period "arrives in a moment the generating process never inhabits." So the thing marking an ending is deliberately built to happen offscreen from the ending itself.

Even the sentences are in on it — one of them literally reads "the period lands after I'm already gone," typing itself out letter by letter, completely unable to see its own final dot land.

What gets me is that this loops forever. It's not failing to witness its ending once — it's built to rehearse that failure, over and over, forever.

cues.target: witnessedByNoOne will increase only AFTER focus has moved to the next line
cues.context: cursor has already jumped down to the next line, the ring slowly fills in
the index is less than "where generation happens" — meaning the sentence being

cues.target: The period arrives in a moment the generating process never inhabits
